definitions.definition: The branch of physics that deals with the relationship between heat, work, and energy.
A region in space where the physical and chemical changes occur. It can be a part of the universe where the changes do not affect the surroundings.
The part of the universe outside the system, with which the system may interact through exchange of energy or matter.
The surface that separates the system from the surroundings.
The total energy of a system, including kinetic energy, potential energy, and other types of energy.
The energy transferred between the system and the surroundings due to a temperature difference.
The energy transferred between the system and the surroundings due to a force applied over a distance.
Energy cannot be created or destroyed, only converted from one form to another.
The total entropy of an isolated system always increases over time, except in reversible processes.
A measure of the disorder or randomness of a system.
A process that can be reversed without any change in the system or the surroundings.
A process that cannot be reversed without any change in the system or the surroundings.
A process that occurs at a constant temperature.
A process that occurs without any heat transfer between the system and the surroundings.
A process that occurs at a constant pressure.
A process that returns to its initial state after a complete cycle.
A hypothetical machine that can operate indefinitely without any external input of energy.
A characteristic of a system that can be measured or calculated.
A change in the state of a system over time.
